title_en: What to expect from your first consultation
title_el: What to expect from your first consultation
excerpt_en: A walk through the assessment, the questions we ask, and why we never treat on the first promise.
excerpt_el: A walk through the assessment, the questions we ask, and why we never treat on the first promise.
body_en:
A first consultation at a good clinic is mostly listening. We ask what is bothering you, what you have tried, and what you actually want the outcome to be.

We will not treat you on the same visit unless it is genuinely right. A plan, a price and a cooling-off period come first. That is not caution for its own sake. It is how good aesthetic medicine works.
body_el:
A first consultation at a good clinic is mostly listening. We ask what is bothering you, what you have tried, and what you actually want the outcome to be.

We will not treat you on the same visit unless it is genuinely right. A plan, a price and a cooling-off period come first. That is not caution for its own sake. It is how good aesthetic medicine works.

excerpt_en: What it does, what it does not, and how to spot conservative, well-judged dosing.
excerpt_el: What it does, what it does not, and how to spot conservative, well-judged dosing.
body_en:
Anti-wrinkle treatment relaxes specific muscles so the lines they create soften. Done well, it is invisible. Done badly, it is the frozen look everyone is afraid of.

The difference is dosing and placement. We start conservative, review you at two weeks, and top up only if needed. You can always add a little. You cannot take it away.
body_el:
Anti-wrinkle treatment relaxes specific muscles so the lines they create soften. Done well, it is invisible. Done badly, it is the frozen look everyone is afraid of.

The difference is dosing and placement. We start conservative, review you at two weeks, and top up only if needed. You can always add a little. You cannot take it away.

title_en: How to read a skincare label
title_el: How to read a skincare label
excerpt_en: The actives worth paying for, the claims worth ignoring, and a simple routine that works.
excerpt_el: The actives worth paying for, the claims worth ignoring, and a simple routine that works.
body_en:
Most skincare is marketing. A few ingredients genuinely earn their place: a vitamin C in the morning, a retinoid at night, and an SPF every single day.

Ignore the word natural, ignore most percentages on the front of the box, and build a routine of three or four products you will actually use. Consistency beats complexity.
body_el:
Most skincare is marketing. A few ingredients genuinely earn their place: a vitamin C in the morning, a retinoid at night, and an SPF every single day.

Ignore the word natural, ignore most percentages on the front of the box, and build a routine of three or four products you will actually use. Consistency beats complexity.
